datasets.displayName: EFSA Comprehensive European Food Consumption Database
notes: Governed metadata entry for the EFSA food-consumption infrastructure used to support EU dietary exposure workflows.
This registry entry does not redistribute the underlying proprietary or access-controlled survey extracts.

datasets.displayName: EFSA DietEx dietary exposure support metadata
notes: Governed metadata entry for DietEx as exposure-support tooling around EFSA food-consumption infrastructure.
DietEx is represented separately from PRIMo and is not treated as a submission engine in Dietary MCP.
Governed metadata for chronic general-population cluster diets used in JMPR and related global screening contexts.
Not suitable for children-specific or shorter-than-lifetime exposure assessment.

datasets.displayName: EFSA EU Menu standardised survey programme metadata
notes: Governed programme-level metadata for EFSA's EU Menu effort to standardise food consumption data collection across Member States.
This entry complements the Comprehensive Database registry record and does not redistribute raw survey files.

datasets.displayName: EFSA Comprehensive Food Consumption Database PFAS support metadata
notes: Governed metadata entry showing that EFSA food-consumption infrastructure is part of the review context for PFAS dietary exposure in food.
This is provenance and configuration support only; Dietary MCP does not publish a native PFAS submission engine in v0.1.

datasets.displayName: EC PFAS in food monitoring programme metadata 2022-2025
notes: Governed metadata for the Commission recommendation to monitor PFAS in food from 2022 to 2025.
Published as monitoring-programme context rather than as a public occurrence dataset extract.

datasets.displayName: EFSA Comprehensive Food Consumption Database acrylamide support metadata
notes: Governed metadata entry showing that EFSA food-consumption infrastructure is part of the review context for dietary acrylamide exposure.
This is provenance and configuration support only; Dietary MCP does not publish a native acrylamide submission engine in v0.1.

datasets.displayName: EU acrylamide in food monitoring programme metadata 2019 onward
notes: Governed metadata for EU monitoring of acrylamide in certain foods and transmission of data to EFSA.
Published as monitoring-programme context rather than as a public occurrence dataset extract.

datasets.displayName: EFSA Comprehensive Food Consumption Database BPA support metadata
notes: Governed metadata entry showing that EFSA food-consumption infrastructure forms part of the dietary context for BPA exposure in foodstuffs.
This is provenance and dietary-context support only; Dietary MCP does not publish a native food-contact migration engine in v0.1.

datasets.displayName: EFSA Comprehensive Food Consumption Database cadmium support metadata
notes: Governed metadata entry showing that EFSA food-consumption infrastructure forms part of the dietary exposure context for cadmium in food.
This is provenance and dietary-context support only; Dietary MCP does not publish a native cadmium submission engine in v0.1.

datasets.displayName: EFSA Comprehensive Food Consumption Database lead support metadata
notes: Governed metadata entry showing that EFSA food-consumption infrastructure forms part of the dietary exposure context for lead in food.
This is provenance and dietary-context support only; Dietary MCP does not publish a native lead submission engine in v0.1.

datasets.displayName: EFSA Comprehensive Food Consumption Database inorganic arsenic support metadata
notes: Governed metadata entry showing that EFSA food-consumption infrastructure forms part of the dietary exposure context for inorganic arsenic in food.
This is provenance and dietary-context support only; Dietary MCP does not publish a native inorganic-arsenic submission engine in v0.1.

datasets.displayName: EFSA Comprehensive Food Consumption Database mercury support metadata
notes: Governed metadata entry showing that EFSA food-consumption infrastructure forms part of the dietary exposure context for mercury in food.
This is provenance and dietary-context support only; Dietary MCP does not publish a native mercury submission engine in v0.1.
